timeline: 10:02 - DB connection spikes from 40 to 150 (max capacity)
10:04 - Latency on /checkout endpoint increases to 8.4 seconds
incidents.resolution: Increased max connection pool size from 100 to 250 in checkout configuration and added Redis caching for product metadata.

incidents.rootCause: Memory Leak via Unclosed Stream Readers
timeline: 14:30 - Container memory usage grows from 45% to 98% linearly
incidents.resolution: Fixed unclosed stream in PDF Invoice generator utility. Implemented strict memory limits in Kubernetes deployment files.

timeline: 08:35 - Redis cache memory limit reached (No eviction policy set)
08:40 - Token verification requests fall back directly to DB decrypt routines
08:45 - Auth CPU usage hits 100%, authentication lag reaches 4 seconds
09:10 - Redis eviction policy updated to volatile-lru, Redis restarted
incidents.resolution: Updated Redis deployment configuration to include maxmemory policy as volatile-lru and doubled memory allocation.

recommendations: Increase database connection pooling limits before triggering deploy
Perform stress testing on the checkout endpoint
Delay deployment: an open critical blocker bug exists on checkout-service
Implement a Canary strategy with a 10% traffic split first
Monitor memory usage closely for the first 15 minutes

jiraTickets.summary: Memory leak detected when exporting heavy checkout reports
CSS alignment fix on checkout header

runbooks.title: Resolving Database Connection Pool Saturation
runbooks.content: When db connection pools exhaust, checkout api requests queue and time out. Mitigate by increasing pool size parameters (e.g. pool.max=250), auditing unreturned connections to connection pools, and utilizing Redis cache buffers for repetitive static queries.

runbooks.title: Fixing Out of Memory leaks in NodeJS/TypeScript applications
runbooks.content: Out of memory exceptions are commonly caused by unclosed IO streams, holding massive array objects globally, or circular references. To troubleshoot, review memory profile heapdumps, execute GC manually in sandbox, and guarantee proper disposal of reader/writer instances inside try-finally blocks.

runbooks.title: Addressing Redis Cache Exhaustion
runbooks.content: Redis errors related to memory thresholds are mitigated by updating the maxmemory-policy configuration inside redis.conf. Use volatile-lru or allkeys-lru to allow active eviction of expired keys, avoiding server freezes and fallbacks to slower core databases.
